453	# OutlineWorkspace — the agent's mutable state (checkpointed after every mutation)
454	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
455	
456	@dataclass
457	class OutlineWorkspace:
458	    research_question: str
459	    ev_store: dict[str, dict[str, Any]]              # cp2 rows keyed by evidence_id, S2-stamped
460	    outline_draft: list[Any] = field(default_factory=list)   # list[SectionPlan]
461	    gap_ledger: GapLedger = field(default_factory=GapLedger)
462	    notebook: Optional[AnalysisNotebook] = None
463	    basket_menu: Any = None                            # outline_digest menu (or None)
464	    budgets: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
465	    disclosures: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
466	    # W4: successful execute_python payloads, so a computed value is no longer discarded at
467	    # every exit. EXPLORATORY / NOT RENDERABLE — every row carries renderable=False +
468	    # bar_reason (see _compute_result_check). Telemetry + planner provenance only; this is
469	    # NOT a render path and never reaches a writer prompt.
470	    compute_results: list[dict] = field(default_factory=list)
471	    # W3-render (2026-07-11): run-scoped registry of VERIFIED quantified models, keyed by
472	    # (model_id, spec_hash) exactly as ``strict_verify(quantified_models=...)`` expects. This is
473	    # the ONLY compute-render surface: a number lands here solely by re-derivation through the
474	    # fail-closed ModelSpec lane (verified_compute.run_verified_compute -> build_quantified_spec ->
475	    # execute_quantified_model), and renders solely via its ``[#calc:model:hash:field]`` token,
476	    # which strict_verify force-routes to ``verify_modeled_atom``. Exploratory execute_python output
477	    # (compute_results above, renderable=False) NEVER enters this dict — that separation is the
478	    # faithfulness invariant (a derived number can never reach the [#ev]/[CITE] render path).
479	    quantified_models: dict = field(default_factory=dict)  # (model_id, spec_hash) -> QuantifiedResult
480	    # MOAT DETERMINISTIC EMISSION (2026-07-11): the render-ready [#calc:] claim SENTENCES the agent
481	    # actually derived through ``verified_compute``, so the FULL-CORPUS composer can inject them
482	    # into the target section body deterministically (an LLM writer cannot be trusted to copy an
483	    # unguessable spec_hash verbatim). Each record:
484	    #   {"section": <target section title or "" for auto-home>, "sentence": <render_sentence>,
485	    #    "calc_token": <[#calc:model:hash:field]>, "input_ev_ids": [<ev_id>, ...]}
486	    # Populated by the ``verified_compute`` tool alongside the ``quantified_models`` registration.
487	    # The sentence is NON-RENDERING on its own: it only survives strict_verify if that same
488	    # registry verifies its token (verify_modeled_atom), so emission stays fail-closed and a
489	    # derived number STILL cannot reach the [#ev:]/[CITE:] path.
490	    computed_claims: list[dict] = field(default_factory=list)
491	    turn: int = 0
492	    started_monotonic: float = field(default_factory=time.monotonic)
493	    checkpoint_dir: Optional[str] = None
494	    total_input_tokens: int = 0
495	    total_output_tokens: int = 0
496	    # Iter-2 fix: the deliverable's REQUIRED section titles (empty => no structure lock, the
497	    # common case for the free-form acceptance/real-corpus runs). When non-empty, the outline's
498	    # section STRUCTURE is governed by the caller (S4 ORCH-2 PUSH 1 / `_call_outline`'s own
499	    # `required_sections` conform already enforced this at SEED time) and this module must never
500	    # silently add a 5th section behind the caller's back — see `_tool_update_outline` and the
501	    # auto-assign fallback in `OutlineAgent._execute_tool`.
502	    required_titles: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
503	    # RACE-FLOOR fix (2026-07-12, this wheel): the corpus-DERIVED thematic-coverage floor. The SEED
504	    # outline (built by ``_call_outline`` from the evidence/baskets — query-agnostic, NOT a hardcoded
505	    # task-72 structure) is the corpus's own theme decomposition. Two byte-identical renders diverged
506	    # (RACE 0.4447 vs 0.3518) because the agentic loop, over more turns, freely issued ``merge`` ops
507	    # that COLLAPSED distinct corpus themes (e.g. Wage-Inequality, Policy) into fewer, thinner
508	    # sections — pure non-determinism that thinned comprehensiveness. This floor caps NET thematic
509	    # reduction: the loop may still split / add / retitle / reassign / search (all coverage-improving),
510	    # but a ``merge`` that would drop the live section count below the seed count is DEFERRED as a
511	    # disclosed no-op. GENERAL (floor = seed's own section count), faithfulness-NEUTRAL (pure
512	    # structural placement; strict_verify / NLI / [#calc] lane untouched). 0 => no floor (legacy).
513	    min_sections: int = 0
514	    # Iter-3 P0 fix (real-corpus THINNED run: 8 search_more_evidence calls, 31 genuinely NEW
515	    # fetched rows, ZERO landed in any required section — the whole retrieval budget was spent
516	    # re-chasing the SAME unhomeable section label, e.g. "Summary Table", under 8 differently
517	    # worded aspects that each failed the (section,aspect) paraphrase dedup because the ASPECT
518	    # text varied enough while the SECTION stayed constant). Once a section label is proven
519	    # unhomeable (checklist- or agent-initiated), it is banked here (normalized) and vetoed —
520	    # cheaply, before any real fetch — for the rest of the run, regardless of aspect rewording.
521	    unhomeable_sections: set = field(default_factory=set)
522	    # S3 FEED (Research Planning Gate ↔ outline agent seam). All default to the
523	    # inert/empty value so a workspace built without a gate artifact (every
524	    # champion caller today) is BYTE-IDENTICAL: no scope threaded into the gap
525	    # search, no term-ledger validation on update_outline, no seeded gaps.
526	    #
527	    # ``gate_research_frame`` / ``gate_gap_protocol``: THE BUG FIX. The gate's
528	    # retrieval scope (the SAME ``research_frame`` + anchor ``protocol`` used by
529	    # initial retrieval), threaded into ``_tool_search_more_evidence``'s
530	    # ``run_live_retrieval`` call so the outline-stage gap search stays IN SCOPE
531	    # instead of running unscoped (both designers found this bug). None => the
532	    # champion ``run_live_retrieval`` defaults (research_frame=None/protocol=None).
533	    gate_research_frame: Any = None
534	    gate_gap_protocol: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None
535	    # ``contract_hash``: the pinned artifact's contract SHA (provenance only — the
536	    # resolved outline records which gate seed it refined). "" => no gate.
537	    contract_hash: str = ""
538	    # ``term_ledger``: {binding_term_owners, locked_titles, ordered_titles,
539	    # binding_term_ids}. When non-empty, ``_tool_update_outline`` rejects a
540	    # revision that drops an explicit title lock or the LAST owner of a binding
541	    # term (build via ``outline_gate_feed.build_term_ledger``). Empty => no ledger
542	    # validation (champion behavior).
543	    term_ledger: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
544	    _checkpoint_seq: int = 0
545	
546	    def next_ev_offset(self) -> int:
547	        """Highest numeric suffix across current ev_ids, +1. Rows that don't parse as
548	        ``ev_<digits>`` are ignored for the offset computation (never crash the offset)."""
549	        mx = -1
550	        for eid in self.ev_store.keys():
551	            tail = str(eid).rsplit("_", 1)[-1]
552	            if tail.isdigit():
553	                mx = max(mx, int(tail))
554	        return mx + 1
555	
556	    def existing_urls(self) -> set[str]:
557	        urls: set[str] = set()
558	        for row in self.ev_store.values():
559	            if isinstance(row, dict):
560	                u = str(row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "").strip()
561	                if u:
562	                    urls.add(u)
563	        return urls
564	
565	    def disclose(self, text: str) -> None:
566	        self.disclosures.append(text)
567	        logger.info("[outline_agent] %s", text)
568	
569	    def elapsed_seconds(self) -> float:
570	        return time.monotonic() - self.started_monotonic
571	
572	    def checkpoint(self, event: str) -> None:
573	        """Best-effort checkpoint-after-every-mutation (crash-resume). NEVER raises — a
574	        checkpoint failure must not abort the loop (mirrors ``write_cp4_outline_snapshot``'s
575	        own best-effort contract one layer up)."""
576	        if not self.checkpoint_dir:
577	            return
578	        try:
579	            from src.polaris_graph.generator.outline_checkpoint import (  # noqa: PLC0415
580	                build_cp4_payload, write_cp4_outline_snapshot,
581	            )
582	            self._checkpoint_seq += 1
583	            plans_dicts = [_plan_to_dict(p) for p in self.outline_draft]
584	            payload = build_cp4_payload(
585	                question_sha="",
586	                upstream=None,
587	                run_config_sha="",
588	                flag_slate={"PG_OUTLINE_AGENT": "1", "event": str(event)},
589	                adjustments_applied=None,
590	                final_plans=plans_dicts,
591	                revision_audit={
592	                    "gap_ledger": self.gap_ledger.as_list(),
593	                    "disclosures": list(self.disclosures),
594	                    "turn": self.turn,
595	                    "checkpoint_seq": self._checkpoint_seq,
596	                    "notebook_steps": (
597	                        [
598	                            {
599	                                "tool_name": s.tool_name,
600	                                "reasoning": s.reasoning[:200],
601	                                "success": s.result.success,
602	                            }
603	                            for s in self.notebook.steps
604	                        ] if self.notebook is not None else []
605	                    ),
606	                },
607	                digest_stats={
608	                    "ev_store_size": len(self.ev_store),
609	                    "elapsed_seconds": round(self.elapsed_seconds(), 1),
610	                },
611	            )
612	            write_cp4_outline_snapshot(self.checkpoint_dir, payload)
613	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — checkpoint is best-effort, never fatal
614	            logger.warning("[outline_agent] checkpoint write skipped (fail-open): %s", exc)
615

617	def _plan_field(plan: Any, key: str, default: Any = "") -> Any:
618	    """Iter-2 fix (found via offline smoke test, not in the original Fable P0/P1 list): plans in
619	    ``workspace.outline_draft`` are NOT a uniform type across the loop's lifetime — the SEED plans
620	    from ``_call_outline`` are ``SectionPlan`` dataclass instances, but ``outline_revise.apply_
621	    revision_ops`` (which BOTH ``update_outline`` and the new iter-2 auto-assign call after every
622	    successful search) always returns plain ``dict`` plans (``RevisionApplyResult.new_plans:
623	    list[dict]``). A bare ``getattr(p, 'title', default)`` silently returns ``default`` for a dict
624	    (dicts have no attribute access) — so the FIRST time any update_outline/auto-assign op ran,
625	    every downstream title lookup in THIS module (decide's outline summary, the checklist's
626	    section block, ``inspect_basket``'s assignment check, auto-assign's own section-title
627	    resolver) would have silently gone blank, breaking the very mutation this iteration exists to
628	    fix. Mirrors ``outline_revise._plan_get`` exactly (dict-or-attribute duck typing) — reproduced
629	    here rather than imported to keep this module's OFF-path import graph unchanged (LAW VI/V:
630	    small, side-effect-free helper; not worth a cross-module coupling for 4 lines)."""
631	    if isinstance(plan, dict):
632	        return plan.get(key, default)
633	    return getattr(plan, key, default)
634	
635	
636	def _plan_to_dict(plan: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
637	    if isinstance(plan, dict):
638	        return dict(plan)
639	    if dataclasses.is_dataclass(plan):
640	        return dataclasses.asdict(plan)
641	    return {
642	        "title": _plan_field(plan, "title", ""),
643	        "focus": _plan_field(plan, "focus", ""),
644	        "ev_ids": list(_plan_field(plan, "ev_ids", []) or []),
645	        "basket_ids": list(_plan_field(plan, "basket_ids", []) or []),
646	        "archetype": _plan_field(plan, "archetype", ""),
647	        "undersupplied": _plan_field(plan, "undersupplied", False),
648	    }
649

651	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
652	# search_more_evidence — the retrieval pipe as a tool (the sharp seam)
653	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
654	
655	def _sync_llm_bridge(model: str, max_tokens: int) -> Callable[[str], str]:
656	    """Build a SYNCHRONOUS ``str -> str`` callable bridging into the async
657	    ``OpenRouterClient.generate`` — the exact thread+contextvars pattern
658	    ``run_honest_sweep_r3.py``'s ``_topic_llm`` uses (that module is not importable here without
659	    dragging in the whole sweep script, so the ~20-line bridge is reproduced, not re-derived).
660	    Required because ``classify_topic_relevance`` (topic_relevance_gate.py) takes a sync
661	    callable and this module runs inside an ALREADY-RUNNING asyncio loop (the outline agent's
662	    own loop), where a bare ``asyncio.run()`` would raise."""
663	
664	    def _llm(prompt: str) -> str:
665	        import concurrent.futures as _futures
666	        import contextvars as _contextvars
667	
668	        async def _run() -> str:
669	            from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient  # noqa: PLC0415
670	            client = OpenRouterClient(model=model)
671	            try:
672	                resp = await client.generate(prompt=prompt, max_tokens=max_tokens, temperature=0.0)
673	                return (resp.content or "").strip()
674	            finally:
675	                if hasattr(client, "close"):
676	                    try:
677	                        await client.close()
678	                    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — client close is best-effort
679	                        pass
680	
681	        parent_ctx = _contextvars.copy_context()
682	
683	        def _worker() -> str:
684	            return parent_ctx.run(lambda: asyncio.run(_run()))
685	
686	        with _futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1) as pool:
687	            return pool.submit(_worker).result()
688	
689	    return _llm
690	
691	
692	async def _tool_search_more_evidence(
693	    workspace: OutlineWorkspace,
694	    agent_model: str,
695	    *,
696	    section: str = "",
697	    aspect: str = "",
698	    domain: Optional[str] = None,
699	    retrieval_deadline_monotonic: Optional[float] = None,
700	    **_ignored: Any,
701	) -> ToolResult:
702	    """The retrieval pipe as a ReAct tool. Steps (design §): per-todo query derivation
703	    (scope-anchored, ``_is_status_leak`` guard) -> ``run_live_retrieval(anchor_seed=False)`` ->
704	    ``merge_retrieval_results`` -> offset-renumber (hard assert) + URL-dedup vs existing ->
705	    S2 stamp pass (chrome delete + topic-judge fail-open) -> fold into ``ev_store`` -> disclosed
706	    ``ToolResult``. One retrieval in flight at a time (§8.4 — this coroutine is awaited, never
707	    fired concurrently by the driver)."""
708	    aspect_text = str(aspect or "").strip() or str(section or "").strip()
709	    if not aspect_text:
710	        return ToolResult(
711	            success=False, tool_name="search_more_evidence",
712	            markdown="search_more_evidence requires a `section` or `aspect`.",
713	            error="missing_aspect",
714	        )
715	
716	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.fs_researcher_query_gen import (  # noqa: PLC0415
717	        _is_status_leak,
718	    )
719	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient  # noqa: PLC0415
720	
721	    query_derive_max_tokens = _env_int(
722	        "PG_OUTLINE_QUERY_DERIVE_MAX_TOKENS", PG_OUTLINE_QUERY_DERIVE_MAX_TOKENS_DEFAULT,
723	    )
724	    client = OpenRouterClient(model=agent_model)
725	    try:
726	        derive_prompt = (
727	            "Write ONE web-search query for the SUB-TOPIC below, kept STRICTLY within the "
728	            "scope of the RESEARCH QUESTION (carry its subject, domain and key entities; do "
729	            "NOT broaden the query into the sub-topic's generic field). Query only, one line, "
730	            "no quotes.\n\n"
731	            f"RESEARCH QUESTION:\n{workspace.research_question}\n\nSUB-TOPIC:\n{aspect_text}"
732	        )
733	        try:
734	            # Iter-6 P0 fix: same reasoning-pool bound as the checklist/decide calls above —
735	            # this call already fails open on ANY exception (including a truncation), but an
736	            # unbounded-effort reasoning prelude on a dense corpus still needlessly degrades a
737	            # one-line query derivation to the raw aspect text every time; bounding it means the
738	            # derived (scope-anchored) query actually gets used instead of the coarser fallback.
739	            resp = await client.generate(
740	                prompt=derive_prompt, max_tokens=query_derive_max_tokens, temperature=0.2,
741	                reasoning_max_tokens=_reasoning_max_tokens(),
742	            )
743	            derived = (resp.content or "").strip().splitlines()[0].strip().strip('"') if (
744	                resp.content or ""
745	            ).strip() else ""
746	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — query derivation is a small text call; degrade to raw aspect
747	            logger.warning("[outline_agent] query derivation failed, using raw aspect: %s", exc)
748	            derived = ""
749	    finally:
750	        if hasattr(client, "close"):
751	            try:
752	                await client.close()
753	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
754	                pass
755	
756	    query = derived or aspect_text
757	    if _is_status_leak(query, workspace.research_question):
758	        return ToolResult(
759	            success=False, tool_name="search_more_evidence",
760	            markdown=f"Derived query was a status-leak, aborted: {query[:120]!r}",
761	            error="status_leak_aborted",
762	        )
763	
764	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.live_retriever import (  # noqa: PLC0415
765	        LiveRetrievalResult, run_live_retrieval,
766	    )
767	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.fs_researcher_query_gen import (  # noqa: PLC0415
768	        merge_retrieval_results,
769	    )
770	
771	    t0 = time.monotonic()
772	    try:
773	        # BUG FOUND IN W1 LIVE ACCEPTANCE RUN (fixed same-iter): ``anchor_seed=False`` makes
774	        # ``run_live_retrieval`` build ``all_queries`` from ``amplified_queries`` ONLY (the
775	        # verbatim ``research_question`` is NOT auto-fired when the anchor is suppressed — see
776	        # the function's own docstring). The gap query MUST be passed via ``amplified_queries``;
777	        # ``research_question`` stays the WORKSPACE question (scope-validator anchor text) so a
778	        # scoped sub-query is validated against the real topic, not against itself.
779	        # S3 BUG FIX (both designers): thread the gate's retrieval SCOPE into the
780	        # outline-stage gap search. Before this, ``research_frame`` / ``protocol``
781	        # were silently dropped here, so a gap search run under a hard journal /
782	        # English / jurisdiction contract went out UNSCOPED and could dilute a
783	        # correctly-scoped first pass. When the workspace carries no gate scope
784	        # (every champion caller — both stay None/None), this is byte-identical to
785	        # the champion ``run_live_retrieval`` defaults. A HARD-scope frame is never
786	        # a post-fetch filter here: it ROUTES the SAME backends/anchor the initial
787	        # retrieval used, so the gap query keeps that scope from its first fetch.
788	        raw_result = await asyncio.to_thread(
789	            run_live_retrieval,
790	            research_question=workspace.research_question,
791	            amplified_queries=[query],
792	            domain=domain,
793	            anchor_seed=False,
794	            retrieval_deadline_monotonic=retrieval_deadline_monotonic,
795	            research_frame=workspace.gate_research_frame,
796	            protocol=workspace.gate_gap_protocol,
797	        )
798	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a single retrieval round must not crash the loop
799	        return ToolResult(
800	            success=False, tool_name="search_more_evidence",
801	            markdown=f"run_live_retrieval raised for query {query!r}: {exc}",
802	            error=str(exc)[:500],
803	        )
804	    elapsed = time.monotonic() - t0
805	
806	    merged = merge_retrieval_results([raw_result], LiveRetrievalResult)
807	    fetched_rows = list(getattr(merged, "evidence_rows", None) or [])
808	    n_fetched_of = f"{len(fetched_rows)} of {getattr(merged, 'candidates_total', len(fetched_rows))}"
809	
810	    # THE shared fold-in seam (outline/_fold_in.py): url-dedup -> offset-renumber (hard
811	    # id-collision assert) -> S2 stamp/delete -> insert. fetch_url re-enters through the same unit.
812	    fold = await fold_in_fetched_rows(
813	        workspace, fetched_rows,
814	        research_question=workspace.research_question, agent_model=agent_model,
815	    )
816	    kept_rows = fold.kept_rows
817	    n_kept = fold.n_kept
818	    n_deleted = fold.n_deleted
819	    disclosure = (
820	        f"search_more_evidence[{aspect_text[:60]!r}] query={query!r} fetched {n_fetched_of}, "
821	        f"url-dup dropped {fold.n_url_dup}, kept {n_kept}, deleted {n_deleted} "
822	        f"(chrome={len(fold.deleted_chrome)}, off-topic={len(fold.deleted_offtopic)}) in {elapsed:.1f}s"
823	    )
824	    workspace.disclose(disclosure)
825	
826	    md_lines = [f"**{disclosure}**", ""]
827	    for row in kept_rows[:10]:
828	        md_lines.append(
829	            f"- {row.get('evidence_id')}: {str(row.get('title') or '')[:80]} "
830	            f"[CITE:{row.get('evidence_id')}]"
831	        )
832	    if len(kept_rows) > 10:
833	        md_lines.append(f"- ... and {len(kept_rows) - 10} more")
834	
835	    return ToolResult(
836	        success=n_kept > 0,
837	        tool_name="search_more_evidence",
838	        markdown="\n".join(md_lines),
839	        source_evidence_ids=[r["evidence_id"] for r in kept_rows],
840	        error=("" if n_kept > 0 else "no_new_evidence_survived_screen"),
841	    )
842

844	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
845	# inspect_basket

def get(self, section: str, aspect: str) -> Optional[GapTodo]:
        return self._todos.get((str(section or "").strip(), str(aspect or "").strip()))

    def next_pending(self) -> Optional[GapTodo]:
        for todo in self._todos.values():
            if todo.status == "PENDING":
                return todo
        return None

    def mark_in_progress(self, todo: GapTodo) -> None:
        todo.status = "IN_PROGRESS"
        todo.attempts += 1

    def mark_complete(self, todo: GapTodo) -> None:
        todo.status = "COMPLETE"

    def mark_retry_or_unfilled(self, todo: GapTodo, reason: str) -> None:
        """Per-aspect retry: PENDING again while attempts < cap; UNFILLED + disclosed at cap."""
        if todo.attempts >= self._max_retries:
            todo.status = "UNFILLED"
            todo.disclosure = reason
        else:
            todo.status = "PENDING"

    @property
    def pending_count(self) -> int:
        return sum(1 for t in self._todos.values() if t.status == "PENDING")

    @property
    def unfilled(self) -> list[GapTodo]:
        return [t for t in self._todos.values() if t.status == "UNFILLED"]

    @property
    def all_todos(self) -> list[GapTodo]:
        return list(self._todos.values())

    def as_list(self) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
        return [dataclasses.asdict(t) for t in self._todos.values()]

    def summary_for_llm(self) -> str:
        if not self._todos:
            return "  (empty — no gaps identified yet)"
        lines = []
        for t in self._todos.values():
            lines.append(
                f"  [{t.status}] section={t.section!r} aspect={t.aspect!r} "
                f"kind={t.needed_kind} attempts={t.attempts}/{self._max_retries} source={t.source}"
            )
        return "\n".join(lines)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# OutlineWorkspace — the agent's mutable state (checkpointed after every mutation)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@dataclass
class OutlineWorkspace:
    research_question: str
    ev_store: dict[str, dict[str, Any]]              # cp2 rows keyed by evidence_id, S2-stamped
    outline_draft: list[Any] = field(default_factory=list)   # list[SectionPlan]
    gap_ledger: GapLedger = field(default_factory=GapLedger)
    notebook: Optional[AnalysisNotebook] = None
    basket_menu: Any = None                            # outline_digest menu (or None)
    budgets: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    disclosures: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    # W4: successful execute_python payloads, so a computed value is no longer discarded at
    # every exit. EXPLORATORY / NOT RENDERABLE — every row carries renderable=False +
    # bar_reason (see _compute_result_check). Telemetry + planner provenance only; this is
    # NOT a render path and never reaches a writer prompt.
    compute_results: list[dict] = field(default_factory=list)
    # W3-render (2026-07-11): run-scoped registry of VERIFIED quantified models, keyed by
    # (model_id, spec_hash) exactly as ``strict_verify(quantified_models=...)`` expects. This is
    # the ONLY compute-render surface: a number lands here solely by re-derivation through the
    # fail-closed ModelSpec lane (verified_compute.run_verified_compute -> build_quantified_spec ->
    # execute_quantified_model), and renders solely via its ``[#calc:model:hash:field]`` token,
    # which strict_verify force-routes to ``verify_modeled_atom``. Exploratory execute_python output
    # (compute_results above, renderable=False) NEVER enters this dict — that separation is the
    # faithfulness invariant (a derived number can never reach the [#ev]/[CITE] render path).
    quantified_models: dict = field(default_factory=dict)  # (model_id, spec_hash) -> QuantifiedResult
    # MOAT DETERMINISTIC EMISSION (2026-07-11): the render-ready [#calc:] claim SENTENCES the agent
    # actually derived through ``verified_compute``, so the FULL-CORPUS composer can inject them
    # into the target section body deterministically (an LLM writer cannot be trusted to copy an
    # unguessable spec_hash verbatim). Each record:
    #   {"section": <target section title or "" for auto-home>, "sentence": <render_sentence>,
    #    "calc_token": <[#calc:model:hash:field]>, "input_ev_ids": [<ev_id>, ...]}
    # Populated by the ``verified_compute`` tool alongside the ``quantified_models`` registration.
    # The sentence is NON-RENDERING on its own: it only survives strict_verify if that same
    # registry verifies its token (verify_modeled_atom), so emission stays fail-closed and a
    # derived number STILL cannot reach the [#ev:]/[CITE:] path.
    computed_claims: list[dict] = field(default_factory=list)
    turn: int = 0
    started_monotonic: float = field(default_factory=time.monotonic)
    checkpoint_dir: Optional[str] = None
    total_input_tokens: int = 0
    total_output_tokens: int = 0
    # Iter-2 fix: the deliverable's REQUIRED section titles (empty => no structure lock, the
    # common case for the free-form acceptance/real-corpus runs). When non-empty, the outline's
    # section STRUCTURE is governed by the caller (S4 ORCH-2 PUSH 1 / `_call_outline`'s own
    # `required_sections` conform already enforced this at SEED time) and this module must never
    # silently add a 5th section behind the caller's back — see `_tool_update_outline` and the
    # auto-assign fallback in `OutlineAgent._execute_tool`.
    required_titles: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    # RACE-FLOOR fix (2026-07-12, this wheel): the corpus-DERIVED thematic-coverage floor. The SEED
    # outline (built by ``_call_outline`` from the evidence/baskets — query-agnostic, NOT a hardcoded
    # task-72 structure) is the corpus's own theme decomposition. Two byte-identical renders diverged
    # (RACE 0.4447 vs 0.3518) because the agentic loop, over more turns, freely issued ``merge`` ops
    # that COLLAPSED distinct corpus themes (e.g. Wage-Inequality, Policy) into fewer, thinner
    # sections — pure non-determinism that thinned comprehensiveness. This floor caps NET thematic
    # reduction: the loop may still split / add / retitle / reassign / search (all coverage-improving),
    # but a ``merge`` that would drop the live section count below the seed count is DEFERRED as a
    # disclosed no-op. GENERAL (floor = seed's own section count), faithfulness-NEUTRAL (pure
    # structural placement; strict_verify / NLI / [#calc] lane untouched). 0 => no floor (legacy).
    min_sections: int = 0
    # Iter-3 P0 fix (real-corpus THINNED run: 8 search_more_evidence calls, 31 genuinely NEW
    # fetched rows, ZERO landed in any required section — the whole retrieval budget was spent
    # re-chasing the SAME unhomeable section label, e.g. "Summary Table", under 8 differently
    # worded aspects that each failed the (section,aspect) paraphrase dedup because the ASPECT
    # text varied enough while the SECTION stayed constant). Once a section label is proven
    # unhomeable (checklist- or agent-initiated), it is banked here (normalized) and vetoed —
    # cheaply, before any real fetch — for the rest of the run, regardless of aspect rewording.
    unhomeable_sections: set = field(default_factory=set)
    # S3 FEED (Research Planning Gate ↔ outline agent seam). All default to the
    # inert/empty value so a workspace built without a gate artifact (every
    # champion caller today) is BYTE-IDENTICAL: no scope threaded into the gap
    # search, no term-ledger validation on update_outline, no seeded gaps.
    #
    # ``gate_research_frame`` / ``gate_gap_protocol``: THE BUG FIX. The gate's
    # retrieval scope (the SAME ``research_frame`` + anchor ``protocol`` used by
    # initial retrieval), threaded into ``_tool_search_more_evidence``'s
    # ``run_live_retrieval`` call so the outline-stage gap search stays IN SCOPE
    # instead of running unscoped (both designers found this bug). None => the
    # champion ``run_live_retrieval`` defaults (research_frame=None/protocol=None).
    gate_research_frame: Any = None
    gate_gap_protocol: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None
    # ``contract_hash``: the pinned artifact's contract SHA (provenance only — the
    # resolved outline records which gate seed it refined). "" => no gate.
    contract_hash: str = ""
    # ``term_ledger``: {binding_term_owners, locked_titles, ordered_titles,
    # binding_term_ids}. When non-empty, ``_tool_update_outline`` rejects a
    # revision that drops an explicit title lock or the LAST owner of a binding
    # term (build via ``outline_gate_feed.build_term_ledger``). Empty => no ledger
    # validation (champion behavior).
    term_ledger: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    _checkpoint_seq: int = 0

    def next_ev_offset(self) -> int:
        """Highest numeric suffix across current ev_ids, +1. Rows that don't parse as
        ``ev_<digits>`` are ignored for the offset computation (never crash the offset)."""
        mx = -1
        for eid in self.ev_store.keys():
            tail = str(eid).rsplit("_", 1)[-1]
            if tail.isdigit():
                mx = max(mx, int(tail))
        return mx + 1

    def existing_urls(self) -> set[str]:
        urls: set[str] = set()
        for row in self.ev_store.values():
            if isinstance(row, dict):
                u = str(row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "").strip()
                if u:
                    urls.add(u)
        return urls

    def disclose(self, text: str) -> None:
        self.disclosures.append(text)
        logger.info("[outline_agent] %s", text)

    def elapsed_seconds(self) -> float:
        return time.monotonic() - self.started_monotonic

    def checkpoint(self, event: str) -> None:
        """Best-effort checkpoint-after-every-mutation (crash-resume). NEVER raises — a
        checkpoint failure must not abort the loop (mirrors ``write_cp4_outline_snapshot``'s
        own best-effort contract one layer up)."""
        if not self.checkpoint_dir:
            return
        try:
            from src.polaris_graph.generator.outline_checkpoint import (  # noqa: PLC0415
                build_cp4_payload, write_cp4_outline_snapshot,
            )
            self._checkpoint_seq += 1
            plans_dicts = [_plan_to_dict(p) for p in self.outline_draft]
            payload = build_cp4_payload(
                question_sha="",
                upstream=None,
                run_config_sha="",
                flag_slate={"PG_OUTLINE_AGENT": "1", "event": str(event)},
                adjustments_applied=None,
                final_plans=plans_dicts,
                revision_audit={
                    "gap_ledger": self.gap_ledger.as_list(),
                    "disclosures": list(self.disclosures),
                    "turn": self.turn,
                    "checkpoint_seq": self._checkpoint_seq,
                    "notebook_steps": (
                        [
                            {
                                "tool_name": s.tool_name,
                                "reasoning": s.reasoning[:200],
                                "success": s.result.success,
                            }
                            for s in self.notebook.steps
                        ] if self.notebook is not None else []
                    ),
                },
                digest_stats={
                    "ev_store_size": len(self.ev_store),
                    "elapsed_seconds": round(self.elapsed_seconds(), 1),
                },
            )
            write_cp4_outline_snapshot(self.checkpoint_dir, payload)
        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — checkpoint is best-effort, never fatal
            logger.warning("[outline_agent] checkpoint write skipped (fail-open): %s", exc)


def _plan_field(plan: Any, key: str, default: Any = "") -> Any:
    """Iter-2 fix (found via offline smoke test, not in the original Fable P0/P1 list): plans in
    ``workspace.outline_draft`` are NOT a uniform type across the loop's lifetime — the SEED plans
    from ``_call_outline`` are ``SectionPlan`` dataclass instances, but ``outline_revise.apply_
    revision_ops`` (which BOTH ``update_outline`` and the new iter-2 auto-assign call after every
    successful search) always returns plain ``dict`` plans (``RevisionApplyResult.new_plans:
    list[dict]``). A bare ``getattr(p, 'title', default)`` silently returns ``default`` for a dict
    (dicts have no attribute access) — so the FIRST time any update_outline/auto-assign op ran,
    every downstream title lookup in THIS module (decide's outline summary, the checklist's
    section block, ``inspect_basket``'s assignment check, auto-assign's own section-title
    resolver) would have silently gone blank, breaking the very mutation this iteration exists to
    fix. Mirrors ``outline_revise._plan_get`` exactly (dict-or-attribute duck typing) — reproduced
    here rather than imported to keep this module's OFF-path import graph unchanged (LAW VI/V:
    small, side-effect-free helper; not worth a cross-module coupling for 4 lines)."""
    if isinstance(plan, dict):
        return plan.get(key, default)
    return getattr(plan, key, default)


def _plan_to_dict(plan: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
    if isinstance(plan, dict):
        return dict(plan)
    if dataclasses.is_dataclass(plan):
        return dataclasses.asdict(plan)
    return {
        "title": _plan_field(plan, "title", ""),
        "focus": _plan_field(plan, "focus", ""),
        "ev_ids": list(_plan_field(plan, "ev_ids", []) or []),
        "basket_ids": list(_plan_field(plan, "basket_ids", []) or []),
        "archetype": _plan_field(plan, "archetype", ""),
        "undersupplied": _plan_field(plan, "undersupplied", False),
    }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# search_more_evidence — the retrieval pipe as a tool (the sharp seam)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _sync_llm_bridge(model: str, max_tokens: int) -> Callable[[str], str]:
    """Build a SYNCHRONOUS ``str -> str`` callable bridging into the async
    ``OpenRouterClient.generate`` — the exact thread+contextvars pattern
    ``run_honest_sweep_r3.py``'s ``_topic_llm`` uses (that module is not importable here without
    dragging in the whole sweep script, so the ~20-line bridge is reproduced, not re-derived).
    Required because ``classify_topic_relevance`` (topic_relevance_gate.py) takes a sync
    callable and this module runs inside an ALREADY-RUNNING asyncio loop (the outline agent's
    own loop), where a bare ``asyncio.run()`` would raise."""

    def _llm(prompt: str) -> str:
        import concurrent.futures as _futures
        import contextvars as _contextvars

        async def _run() -> str:
            from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient  # noqa: PLC0415
            client = OpenRouterClient(model=model)
            try:
                resp = await client.generate(prompt=prompt, max_tokens=max_tokens, temperature=0.0)
                return (resp.content or "").strip()
            finally:
                if hasattr(client, "close"):
                    try:
                        await client.close()
                    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — client close is best-effort
                        pass

        parent_ctx = _contextvars.copy_context()

        def _worker() -> str:
            return parent_ctx.run(lambda: asyncio.run(_run()))

        with _futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1) as pool:
            return pool.submit(_worker).result()

    return _llm


async def _tool_search_more_evidence(
    workspace: OutlineWorkspace,
    agent_model: str,
    *,
    section: str = "",
    aspect: str = "",
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
    retrieval_deadline_monotonic: Optional[float] = None,
    **_ignored: Any,
) -> ToolResult:
    """The retrieval pipe as a ReAct tool. Steps (design §): per-todo query derivation
    (scope-anchored, ``_is_status_leak`` guard) -> ``run_live_retrieval(anchor_seed=False)`` ->
    ``merge_retrieval_results`` -> offset-renumber (hard assert) + URL-dedup vs existing ->
    S2 stamp pass (chrome delete + topic-judge fail-open) -> fold into ``ev_store`` -> disclosed
    ``ToolResult``. One retrieval in flight at a time (§8.4 — this coroutine is awaited, never
    fired concurrently by the driver)."""
    aspect_text = str(aspect or "").strip() or str(section or "").strip()
    if not aspect_text:
        return ToolResult(
            success=False, tool_name="search_more_evidence",
            markdown="search_more_evidence requires a `section` or `aspect`.",
            error="missing_aspect",
        )

    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.fs_researcher_query_gen import (  # noqa: PLC0415
        _is_status_leak,
    )
    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient  # noqa: PLC0415

    query_derive_max_tokens = _env_int(
        "PG_OUTLINE_QUERY_DERIVE_MAX_TOKENS", PG_OUTLINE_QUERY_DERIVE_MAX_TOKENS_DEFAULT,
    )
    client = OpenRouterClient(model=agent_model)
    try:
        derive_prompt = (
            "Write ONE web-search query for the SUB-TOPIC below, kept STRICTLY within the "
            "scope of the RESEARCH QUESTION (carry its subject, domain and key entities; do "
            "NOT broaden the query into the sub-topic's generic field). Query only, one line, "
            "no quotes.\n\n"
            f"RESEARCH QUESTION:\n{workspace.research_question}\n\nSUB-TOPIC:\n{aspect_text}"
        )
        try:
            # Iter-6 P0 fix: same reasoning-pool bound as the checklist/decide calls above —
            # this call already fails open on ANY exception (including a truncation), but an
            # unbounded-effort reasoning prelude on a dense corpus still needlessly degrades a
            # one-line query derivation to the raw aspect text every time; bounding it means the
            # derived (scope-anchored) query actually gets used instead of the coarser fallback.
            resp = await client.generate(
                prompt=derive_prompt, max_tokens=query_derive_max_tokens, temperature=0.2,
                reasoning_max_tokens=_reasoning_max_tokens(),
            )
            derived = (resp.content or "").strip().splitlines()[0].strip().strip('"') if (
                resp.content or ""
            ).strip() else ""
        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — query derivation is a small text call; degrade to raw aspect
            logger.warning("[outline_agent] query derivation failed, using raw aspect: %s", exc)
            derived = ""
    finally:
        if hasattr(client, "close"):
            try:
                await client.close()
            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
                pass

    query = derived or aspect_text
    if _is_status_leak(query, workspace.research_question):
        return ToolResult(
            success=False, tool_name="search_more_evidence",
            markdown=f"Derived query was a status-leak, aborted: {query[:120]!r}",
            error="status_leak_aborted",
        )

    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.live_retriever import (  # noqa: PLC0415
        LiveRetrievalResult, run_live_retrieval,
    )
    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.fs_researcher_query_gen import (  # noqa: PLC0415
        merge_retrieval_results,
    )

    t0 = time.monotonic()
    try:
        # BUG FOUND IN W1 LIVE ACCEPTANCE RUN (fixed same-iter): ``anchor_seed=False`` makes
        # ``run_live_retrieval`` build ``all_queries`` from ``amplified_queries`` ONLY (the
        # verbatim ``research_question`` is NOT auto-fired when the anchor is suppressed — see
        # the function's own docstring). The gap query MUST be passed via ``amplified_queries``;
        # ``research_question`` stays the WORKSPACE question (scope-validator anchor text) so a
        # scoped sub-query is validated against the real topic, not against itself.
        # S3 BUG FIX (both designers): thread the gate's retrieval SCOPE into the
        # outline-stage gap search. Before this, ``research_frame`` / ``protocol``
        # were silently dropped here, so a gap search run under a hard journal /
        # English / jurisdiction contract went out UNSCOPED and could dilute a
        # correctly-scoped first pass. When the workspace carries no gate scope
        # (every champion caller — both stay None/None), this is byte-identical to
        # the champion ``run_live_retrieval`` defaults. A HARD-scope frame is never
        # a post-fetch filter here: it ROUTES the SAME backends/anchor the initial
        # retrieval used, so the gap query keeps that scope from its first fetch.
        raw_result = await asyncio.to_thread(
            run_live_retrieval,
            research_question=workspace.research_question,
            amplified_queries=[query],
            domain=domain,
            anchor_seed=False,
            retrieval_deadline_monotonic=retrieval_deadline_monotonic,
            research_frame=workspace.gate_research_frame,
            protocol=workspace.gate_gap_protocol,
        )
    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a single retrieval round must not crash the loop
        return ToolResult(
            success=False, tool_name="search_more_evidence",
            markdown=f"run_live_retrieval raised for query {query!r}: {exc}",
            error=str(exc)[:500],
        )
    elapsed = time.monotonic() - t0

    merged = merge_retrieval_results([raw_result], LiveRetrievalResult)
    fetched_rows = list(getattr(merged, "evidence_rows", None) or [])
    n_fetched_of = f"{len(fetched_rows)} of {getattr(merged, 'candidates_total', len(fetched_rows))}"

    # THE shared fold-in seam (outline/_fold_in.py): url-dedup -> offset-renumber (hard
    # id-collision assert) -> S2 stamp/delete -> insert. fetch_url re-enters through the same unit.
    fold = await fold_in_fetched_rows(
        workspace, fetched_rows,
        research_question=workspace.research_question, agent_model=agent_model,
    )
    kept_rows = fold.kept_rows
    n_kept = fold.n_kept
    n_deleted = fold.n_deleted
    disclosure = (
        f"search_more_evidence[{aspect_text[:60]!r}] query={query!r} fetched {n_fetched_of}, "
        f"url-dup dropped {fold.n_url_dup}, kept {n_kept}, deleted {n_deleted} "
        f"(chrome={len(fold.deleted_chrome)}, off-topic={len(fold.deleted_offtopic)}) in {elapsed:.1f}s"
    )
    workspace.disclose(disclosure)

    md_lines = [f"**{disclosure}**", ""]
    for row in kept_rows[:10]:
        md_lines.append(
            f"- {row.get('evidence_id')}: {str(row.get('title') or '')[:80]} "
            f"[CITE:{row.get('evidence_id')}]"
        )
    if len(kept_rows) > 10:
        md_lines.append(f"- ... and {len(kept_rows) - 10} more")

    return ToolResult(
        success=n_kept > 0,
        tool_name="search_more_evidence",
        markdown="\n".join(md_lines),
        source_evidence_ids=[r["evidence_id"] for r in kept_rows],
        error=("" if n_kept > 0 else "no_new_evidence_survived_screen"),
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# inspect_basket
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _tool_list_tools(
    registry: "ToolRegistry", *, name: str = "", category: str = "", **_ignored: Any,
) -> ToolResult:
    """Meta-tool: return the FULL spec (description + params) of ONE tool by ``name``, or a one-line
    listing of every tool in ``category``. The on-demand discovery path for the decide menu's
    collapsed non-core index (redesign 'Scaling the decide step past 20 tools'). No LLM, no network."""
    nm = str(name or "").strip()
    cat = str(category or "").strip().lower()
    if nm:
        tool = registry.get_tool(nm)
        if tool is None:
            return ToolResult(
                success=False, tool_name="list_tools",
                markdown=f"No tool named {nm!r}.", error="tool_not_found",
            )
        md = "\n".join(registry._describe_full(tool, True))  # noqa: SLF001 — same package intent
        return ToolResult(success=True, tool_name="list_tools", markdown=md)
    matches = [
        t for t in (registry.get_tool(n) for n in registry.available_tools(True))
        if t is not None and (not cat or cat in [str(x).lower() for x in (t.tags or [])])
    ]
    if not matches:
        return ToolResult(
            success=False, tool_name="list_tools",
            markdown=f"No tools in category {cat!r}." if cat else "No tools registered.",
            error="no_tools_in_category",
        )
    lines = [f"**{len(matches)} tool(s)"
             f"{f' in [{cat}]' if cat else ''}:**"]
    for t in sorted(matches, key=lambda x: x.name):
        lines.append(f"- {t.name}: {t.description.split('. ')[0][:100]}")
    return ToolResult(success=True, tool_name="list_tools", markdown="\n".join(lines))


async def _tool_inspect_basket(
    workspace: OutlineWorkspace, *, basket_id: str = "", **_ignored: Any,

    """Pure read of the cp3 basket digest — members, corroboration, whether every member is
    already assigned to a section. No LLM, no network."""
    menu = workspace.basket_menu

            success=False, tool_name="inspect_basket",

    members = list(getattr(menu, "basket_member_ev_ids", {}).get(basket_id, []) or [])

    if not members:
        return ToolResult(
            success=False, tool_name="inspect_basket",
            markdown=f"Basket {basket_id!r} not found in the digest.",

921	async def _tool_update_outline(
922	    workspace: OutlineWorkspace, *, ops: Any = None, **_ignored: Any,
923	) -> ToolResult:
924	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.outline_revise import (  # noqa: PLC0415
925	        apply_revision_ops, parse_revision_ops,
926	    )
927	
928	    if ops is None:
929	        return ToolResult(
930	            success=False, tool_name="update_outline",
931	            markdown="update_outline requires `ops` (a revision op list).",
932	            error="missing_ops",
933	        )
934	    allowed_ev_ids = set(workspace.ev_store.keys())
935	    plan_titles = [str(_plan_field(p, "title", "")) for p in workspace.outline_draft]
936	    raw = ops if isinstance(ops, (dict, str)) else {"ops": ops}
937	    parse_result = parse_revision_ops(
938	        raw, allowed_ev_ids=allowed_ev_ids, plan_titles=plan_titles,
939	    )
940	    if parse_result.parse_failed or not parse_result.ops:
941	        return ToolResult(
942	            success=False, tool_name="update_outline",
943	            markdown=(
944	                f"No ops applied (parse_failed={parse_result.parse_failed}, "
945	                f"rejected={parse_result.rejected})."
946	            ),
947	            error="no_ops_applied",
948	        )
949	
950	    # Iter-2 fix: thread the deliverable's required-structure lock (if any) into apply — mirrors
951	    # `scripts/orchestrator_lab/outline_lab.py`'s `_required` threading. Without this, a
952	    # required_sections-governed outline (e.g. the DRB-72 4-section canonical structure) could
953	    # silently grow a 5th section via an agent-issued `add` op, breaking the caller's exact-N-in-
954	    # order structural contract that `_call_outline` already conformed the seed to.
955	    # Snapshot the pre-op section titles so the S3 term-ledger guard can compare
956	    # them against the post-op set (below). Cheap; only used when a ledger exists.
957	    _titles_before = [str(_plan_field(p, "title", "")) for p in workspace.outline_draft]
958	
959	    apply_result = apply_revision_ops(
960	        workspace.outline_draft, parse_result,
961	        required_titles=(workspace.required_titles or None),
962	        min_sections=(workspace.min_sections or 0),
963	    )
964	
965	    # ── S3 term-ledger guard: reject a revision that drops an EXPLICIT title lock
966	    # or the LAST owner of a binding term (design §6). This is a SECOND, coverage-
967	    # aware guard on top of ``apply_revision_ops``'s ``required_titles`` structural
968	    # lock: the required-titles lock protects an exact-N-in-order structure, while
969	    # the term ledger protects an explicit heading / a binding requirement's last
970	    # home even when no full structure lock is present. When the workspace carries
971	    # no ledger (every champion caller — ``term_ledger`` empty) this is a no-op and
972	    # byte-identical. On a violation the WHOLE op set is refused (the seed stands),
973	    # disclosed, never silently applied — the loop may retry a compliant revision.
974	    if workspace.term_ledger:
975	        try:
976	            from src.polaris_graph.outline.outline_gate_feed import (  # noqa: PLC0415
977	                validate_revision_against_ledger,
978	            )
979	            _titles_after = [str(d.get("title", "")) for d in apply_result.new_plans]
980	            _violations = validate_revision_against_ledger(
981	                workspace.term_ledger,
982	                titles_before=_titles_before,
983	                titles_after=_titles_after,
984	            )
985	        except Exception as _led_exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — guard is additive, never fatal
986	            _violations = []
987	            logger.warning(
988	                "[outline_agent] S3 ledger guard skipped (fail-open): %s", _led_exc,
989	            )
990	        if _violations:
991	            workspace.disclose(
992	                "update_outline REFUSED: revision would violate the pinned contract "
993	                f"term ledger ({'; '.join(_violations)}) — keeping the prior outline"
994	            )
995	            return ToolResult(
996	                success=False, tool_name="update_outline",
997	                markdown=(
998	                    "No ops applied — the revision would drop an explicit title lock "
999	                    "or the last owner of a binding contract term: "
1000	                    + "; ".join(_violations)
1001	                ),
1002	                error="contract_term_ledger_violation",
1003	            )
1004	    # Iter-2 fix (found via offline smoke test): ``apply_revision_ops`` ALWAYS returns
1005	    # ``list[dict]`` (``outline_revise`` is dict-native; it is not currently wired into
1006	    # production composition at all — grep confirms its only callers before this module were
1007	    # the offline ``outline_lab.py`` harness). But THIS module's ``workspace.outline_draft`` is
1008	    # handed straight back to ``generate_multi_section_report`` as ``parse_result.plans``, and
1009	    # every downstream composition site (M-44 primary injection, section-budget code, the
1010	    # manifest's ``[p.title for p in multi.outline]``, etc.) does direct ``.title``/``.ev_ids``
1011	    # ATTRIBUTE access on real ``SectionPlan`` dataclass instances. Handing dicts downstream
1012	    # would ``AttributeError`` the first real composition run the moment any update_outline (or
1013	    # the iter-2 auto-assign) op fires. Reconcile back to ``SectionPlan`` HERE — the one seam
1014	    # where outline_revise's dict world meets the rest of the pipeline's dataclass world — so
1015	    # every OTHER call site in this module (and every downstream consumer) can keep doing plain
1016	    # ``SectionPlan`` attribute access unconditionally.
1017	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.multi_section_generator import SectionPlan  # noqa: PLC0415
1018	    workspace.outline_draft = [
1019	        SectionPlan(
1020	            title=str(d.get("title", "")),
1021	            focus=str(d.get("focus", "")),
1022	            ev_ids=list(d.get("ev_ids", []) or []),
1023	            archetype=str(d.get("archetype", "")),
1024	            undersupplied=bool(d.get("undersupplied", False)),
1025	            basket_ids=list(d.get("basket_ids", []) or []),
1026	        )
1027	        for d in apply_result.new_plans
1028	    ]
1029	
1030	    # Route the reviser's gap_queries into the shared gap ledger (design §).
1031	    for gq in (parse_result.gap_queries or []):
1032	        workspace.gap_ledger.add(
1033	            section="(unassigned)", aspect=str(gq), needed_kind="coverage", source="agent",
1034	        )
1035	
1036	    md = (
1037	        f"Applied {len(apply_result.applied_ops)} op(s); recompose={apply_result.recompose_titles}; "
1038	        f"kept={len(apply_result.kept_titles)}; rejected={len(parse_result.rejected)}; "
1039	        f"deferred={len(apply_result.deferred_ops)}; gap_queries routed to ledger: "
1040	        f"{len(parse_result.gap_queries or [])}"
1041	    )
1042	    workspace.disclose(f"update_outline: {md}")
1043	    return ToolResult(success=True, tool_name="update_outline", markdown=md)

1054	class OutlineAgent:
1055	    """The per-turn decide/execute loop over the outline workspace. Mirrors the SHAPE of
1056	    ``ReactAnalysisAgent._run_react`` (react_agent.py:690) — decide -> execute -> record ->
1057	    check-stop — without inheriting from it (design: WIRING, not building)."""
1058	
1059	    def __init__(
1060	        self,
1061	        workspace: OutlineWorkspace,
1062	        *,
1063	        agent_model: Optional[str] = None,
1064	        max_turns: Optional[int] = None,
1065	        wall_seconds: Optional[int] = None,
1066	        domain: Optional[str] = None,
1067	        retrieval_deadline_monotonic: Optional[float] = None,
1068	    ):
1069	        self.workspace = workspace
1070	        self.agent_model = agent_model or outliner_agent_model()
1071	        self.max_turns = max_turns if max_turns is not None else _max_turns()
1072	        self.wall_seconds = wall_seconds if wall_seconds is not None else _wall_seconds()
1073	        self.domain = domain
1074	        # Iter-2 P1-3 fix: the OUTER while-loop only checked the wall BETWEEN turns, so a
1075	        # single in-flight ``search_more_evidence`` call could itself run well past the wall
1076	        # (observed: 218s overshoot). ``run_live_retrieval`` already has its own internal
1077	        # mid-fetch wall defense (WALL-03, ``retrieval_deadline_monotonic``) but nothing wired
1078	        # it in — the parameter was accepted here and silently stayed ``None`` (unbounded) on
1079	        # every call. Default it from the SAME wall the outer loop uses, anchored at workspace
1080	        # start, so the in-flight fetch/enrich/classify sub-waits inside a single search call
1081	        # are ALSO bounded by the agent's own wall. An explicit override (e.g. a shared
1082	        # pipeline-wide deadline) still wins.
1083	        self.retrieval_deadline_monotonic = (
1084	            retrieval_deadline_monotonic
1085	            if retrieval_deadline_monotonic is not None
1086	            else workspace.started_monotonic + self.wall_seconds
1087	        )
1088	        self.registry: ToolRegistry = self._build_registry()
1089	        if workspace.notebook is None:
1090	            workspace.notebook = AnalysisNotebook(
1091	                query=workspace.research_question, evidence_ids=list(workspace.ev_store.keys()),
1092	            )

1170	    def _resolve_section_title(self, sec: str) -> Optional[str]:
1171	        """Iter-2 P0-2 helper: resolve a (possibly slightly-off) section name from a tool call
1172	        to the EXACT current outline plan title, so the auto-assign reassign-op always targets a
1173	        real, existing section (never guesses a new one into being). Exact match first, then
1174	        case-insensitive/whitespace-insensitive, then (iter-4 P0-1 fix — a real corpus run
1175	        showed the checklist copy a SHORTENED version of a long real title, e.g. 'Summary
1176	        Table' for the actual title 'Summary Table: Application Cases, Impacts, and Risks by
1177	        Industry/Occupation', and the strict-exact match orphaned the fetched rows) a
1178	        normalized substring-containment or high token-Jaccard match — still IDENTITY
1179	        recognition of an existing section, never an invented one. Returns ``None`` (no guess)
1180	        on no match."""
1181	        sec_norm = str(sec or "").strip()
1182	        if not sec_norm:
1183	            return None
1184	        titles = [str(_plan_field(p, "title", "") or "") for p in self.workspace.outline_draft]
1185	        for title in titles:
1186	            if title == sec_norm:
1187	                return title
1188	        sec_lower = sec_norm.lower()
1189	        for title in titles:
1190	            if title.strip().lower() == sec_lower:
1191	                return title
1192	        sec_canon = _canon_tokens(sec_norm)
1193	        if not sec_canon:
1194	            return None
1195	        threshold = _section_title_fuzzy_threshold()
1196	        best_title: Optional[str] = None
1197	        best_score = 0.0
1198	        for title in titles:
1199	            title_lower = title.strip().lower()
1200	            if title_lower and sec_lower and (sec_lower in title_lower or title_lower in sec_lower):
1201	                return title
1202	            score = _jaccard(sec_canon, _canon_tokens(title))
1203	            if score > best_score:
1204	                best_title, best_score = title, score
1205	        if best_title is not None and best_score >= threshold:
1206	            return best_title
1207	        return None
1208	
1209	    def _best_remap_title(self, aspect: str) -> Optional[str]:
1210	        """Iter-4 P0-1 fix: when a proposed section label matches NO current outline title —
1211	        not even fuzzily via ``_resolve_section_title`` — do not treat the underlying facet as
1212	        automatically unhomeable. On the real DRB-72 AI-labor corpus the checklist's ONLY
1213	        candidate gap was a phantom 'Summary Table' section describing facets (specific
1214	        industries/occupations, impacts, risks) that are each genuinely topical to one of the
1215	        FOUR LOCKED sections the deliverable actually has — vetoing the whole line as a
1216	        'deliverable-level formatting request' discarded real, retrievable content gaps
1217	        instead of routing them. Mechanical (never-LLM) token-overlap: score the aspect text
1218	        against each existing section's OWN topic (title + focus, since focus was itself
1219	        derived from the research question by the seed outline call, so this stays
1220	        question-agnostic — no hardcoded domain vocabulary). This is a ROUTING decision, not a
1221	        quality verdict about the corpus (§-1.3.1 — 'weight/route, don't drop'). Returns
1222	        ``None`` (caller falls back to UNFILLED, honestly disclosed) if nothing clears the
1223	        (deliberately low) floor."""
1224	        aspect_canon = _canon_tokens(aspect)
1225	        if not aspect_canon:
1226	            return None
1227	        threshold = _section_remap_jaccard_threshold()
1228	        best_title: Optional[str] = None
1229	        best_score = 0.0
1230	        for p in self.workspace.outline_draft:
1231	            title = str(_plan_field(p, "title", "") or "").strip()
1232	            if not title:
1233	                continue
1234	            focus = str(_plan_field(p, "focus", "") or "")
1235	            score = _jaccard(aspect_canon, _canon_tokens(f"{title} {focus}"))
1236	            if score > best_score:
1237	                best_title, best_score = title, score
1238	        if best_title is not None and best_score >= threshold:
1239	            return best_title
1240	        return None

1246

1248	        # Redesign "Scaling the decide step past 20 tools": CORE-in-full + a categorized one-line
1249	        # INDEX of the rest once the registry grows past the (env-tunable, LAW VI) threshold. At/below
1250	        # it the menu is byte-identical to the full listing (today's ~14-tool registry prints in full).
1251	        # Default 60: per the redesign the full listing "holds to ~60 tools (~2k tokens, trivial at
1252	        # glm-5.2's 1M ctx)", so today's ~22-tool registry still prints IN FULL (byte-identical live
1253	        # behavior). The CORE+index collapse arms automatically once the MCP layer grows the registry
1254	        # past 60 — no code change, just the env seat.

1261	        # never reached the planner — while success also meant no gap was recorded, so

1270	            f"ev_ids, focus={str(_plan_field(p, 'focus', ''))[:80]!r}"
1271	            for p in self.workspace.outline_draft
1272	        ) or "  (no outline yet)"

1274	        ledger_is_empty = not self.workspace.gap_ledger.all_todos

1279	            if ledger_is_empty else ""

1284	            f"GAP LEDGER:\n{self.workspace.gap_ledger.summary_for_llm()}\n{empty_ledger_note}\n"

        source_evidence_ids=members,
